title: homalobus amphidoxus
intro: Homalobus amphidoxus, a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, is also known as the poison tree or poison wood. Native to tropical regions, it has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as an anthelmintic against parasitic worms. In herbal preparations, Homalobus amphidoxus is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, although caution is advised due to its toxic properties and potential for skin irritation.
